title: Chapter 366 – Peerless Master
content:
Jeong Yeon-shin opened his mouth.

"Stop right there."

His voice, naturally infused with the Luminous Wheel Energy, swept through the undergrowth.

Though soft, it carried far. The chirping of nearby insects died away at once.

Some expenditure of energy was unavoidable, but his composure remained unshaken. Especially since his opponents were nothing compared to the breath of the rain dragon.

"...!"

The martial artists who had been charging toward Jeong Yeon-shin only to veer sharply and flee froze mid-stride.

"Damn it!"

"I told you! I said something was off!"

None of them slowed their pace.

If anything, they pushed their lightness skill harder, as though the spirituality dwelling within the Luminous Wheel Energy were pressing down on their very souls.

It would be fair to say they recognized his rank. Just as Jeong Yeon-shin, back in his White and Blue days, had constantly felt the wall posed by his maternal uncle and the other squad leaders of the Divine Sword Group.

'Oil on their weapons. They've been moving through cities.'

They might be worth interrogating.

Just as his fingers began to twitch.

Middle-aged martial artists pursuing those men from the opposite direction closed in rapidly.

A Taoist bearing a sword and a Buddhist nun wielding an iron staff. The refined, impressive energy waves they exuded were notable. They had been narrowing the gap from the start.

"Damn! They just won't let up...!"

"Scatter!"

The five masters who had earlier tried to use Jeong Yeon-shin as a hostage to escape their predicament.

The instant they spotted the black robe with the rough "Desolate" character emblazoned on it, they pivoted at sharp right angles without a second thought.

They were quick-witted — survivors of the Sima Evil Path long enough to have become masters. Their instincts, honed beyond ordinary energy sense, were their pride.

They called themselves the Five Heroes of Passion Protection.

Known throughout Sichuan's martial world for their respectable skill with weapons, they had banded together to endure the famine sweeping the martial world.

To crush the skulls of those who played hero on full stomachs. To gain an advantage when competing against other martial artists for plunder.

The Five Heroes of Passion Protection were formidable. Their coordination was quite good, too.

Good enough to withdraw before the monstrous figures of the Nine Sects could reach them. Before that, they had even severed the limbs of White-rank martial artists from Desolate Fortress's Sichuan branch and several promising rising talents.

And so the fact that they had just changed course was, in truth, a stroke of good fortune for the masters pursuing them.

After all, there was no need to waste energy on men they were about to capture anyway.

"Fourth Uncle, over there...!"

"Divine Nun Zi Wei mustn't hold back her techniques...!"

Clear voices rang out sporadically from the lower stretch of the road.

Voices filled with trust. Behind the sharp, potent momentum characteristic of rising talents from renowned righteous sects, the middle-aged Taoist and Buddhist nun stamped the ground.

Woong!

Dust erupted from beneath the heel of the Taoist in blue robes. Flowing Name Float — the divine skill of the Qingcheng Sect.

His form, which should have been gentle with Taoist mysteries, carved through space at tremendous speed, leaving a long trail of dust.

The Buddhist nun gripping the iron staff kept pace without falling behind in the slightest. Hers was the Great Stillness Buddha Shadow Body, famed as Emei Sect's divine skill.

A dark shadow flickered across her back, generating an impossibly powerful wave of propulsive energy.

The two closed the distance to the Five Heroes of Passion Protection in an instant.

The five martial artists, sensing the overwhelming energy bearing down on them, threw themselves forward in terror. A technique for evading overhead strikes.

Swaaak—!

A chilling sound tore through the air, and dozens of severed strands of hair drifted upward.

The sword wind that had grazed the exact spot where five necks had been. A strike extended from the Taoist's blade.

"You insane — ! That's Qingcheng hand technique for you!"

"Hey, you mad Taoist! Does your sect teach you to kill without hesitation?!"

The Five Heroes, who had scattered and rolled across the ground, shouted whatever came to mind. Some outright cursed.

Their words and actions held no particular meaning.

Destroy evil and eliminate demons. Break falsehood and reveal truth.

These were originally sayings of the Nine Great Sects with their Buddhist and Taoist affiliations.

Apart from Shaolin Temple on Mount Song, hardly any of the Nine Sects showed mercy to evil sects. Their compassion extended only to the common people.

The Five Heroes of Passion Protection knew this well. They simply needed a moment to catch their breath.

Kwang!

Emei Sect's Divine Nun Zi Wei gave them no such respite.

Unlike the stone staffs carried by monks who don't cultivate martial arts, the staffs borne by Emei's warrior monks were forged from iron, capable of channeling limitless internal energy.

Each time it hammered the ground, targeting the bodies of the Five Heroes as they rolled swiftly in every direction, the earth shuddered as if struck by an earthquake.

Kwang! Kwang! Kwaaaang!

"Amitabha! Amitabha!"

The Buddhist invocation erupted like a war cry. The middle-aged nun's crimson monk's robe billowed violently, the fabric cracking with every swing.

The iron staff descended with relentless, terrifying force, spawning a forest of shadows, and each impact sent dust exploding upward in reverse.

The protective energy of the Five Heroes — well-refined enough to emit a faint glow — crumbled piece by piece with a sound like stone slabs shattering.

The middle-aged Qingcheng swordsman who had launched the opening strike now stood perfectly still.

Whenever the Five Heroes showed signs of rolling toward another escape route, his hand would blur, and simultaneously a blue sword light would bloom like clouds, bursting one blood vessel after another.

Emei Sect and Qingcheng Sect. The Nine Sects of Sichuan Province.

Their caliber was simply different.

One by one, the five fell with low groans, utterly helpless.

None died instantly, but all five had their arms crushed and their legs perforated. Their limb meridians appeared to have ruptured.

'Emei Sect's Extinguishing Distress Thousand Sleeve Staff. Qingcheng Sect's Azure Cloud Sword Technique.'

Jeong Yeon-shin had been standing about ten paces away, watching the scene in something close to a daze.

The Jeong family estate sat in Xinye County, Henan Province — a place where one could hardly avoid encountering the lofty reputation of Shaolin Temple from childhood.

Jeong Yeon-shin, too, had harbored a vague admiration for Shaolin's warrior monks. This was especially true because he'd had no companions to play with.

When he played alone, swinging tree branches and the like, the immortals of the Nine Sects in his imagination somehow always became Bodhidharma or Zhang Sanfeng.

Transcendent figures who knew everything.

A monk flashing fierce eyes and beating people with an iron staff didn't exist in that world. He had never imagined a Taoist unleashing killing sword techniques, either.

'They must have their reasons...?'

He felt vaguely disappointed. As though a precious memory had been dented by staff impressions. It was then.

The Buddhist nun and Taoist, having finished examining the now-critical Five Heroes of Passion Protection, raised their heads. Toward Jeong Yeon-shin.

Swoosh.

As their gazes met, they slowly raised their hands in greeting.

Despite the considerable age difference between them and Jeong Yeon-shin, they offered a Buddhist palm-press and a martial salute first. Their bearing was elegant — nothing like the hand techniques they had displayed moments ago.

"I am Zi Wei. From Emei."

"Primordial Beginning stabilizes and calms... Forgive me for not greeting you properly amid the commotion. This humble Taoist is Qing Yin."

Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin. Masters of the Nine Sects. Their names were known even to Desolate Fortress's main stronghold, which meant they must be thoroughly informed about Sichuan's martial affairs.

"May I ask your name? I haven't heard rumors of anyone like you among the squad leaders of Desolate Fortress..."

The middle-aged Taoist with thick eyebrows, Taoist Master Qing Yin, let his words trail off.

Divine Nun Zi Wei, who appeared to be of similar age, studied Jeong Yeon-shin in silence, her face betraying nothing.

"......"

A gentle breeze swept through the stillness.

Jeong Yeon-shin was inwardly choosing his words. Should he reveal his identity? Ordinarily, the power of Desolate Fortress squad leaders was amplified when their Black-rank identities remained shrouded in mystery.

'Mid-level masters of the Nine Sects...'

They looked calm enough on the surface, but something about them was not entirely at ease.

The intervals between their exhalations and inhalations were still prolonged from when they had struck at the Five Heroes — a sign they hadn't yet lowered their guard.

Ironically, it was Jeong Yeon-shin whose hackles had risen, though from deep disappointment rather than wariness.

It was then.

"Oh? You caught them! These villains!"

"As expected of Fourth Uncle!"

"Just look at that ground. The sword marks of the Azure Cloud Sword Technique... I'm fortunate to witness such enlightenment."

"What technique did the Divine Nun use?"

"Extinguishing Distress Thousand Sleeve Staff."

"Ah, of course. Look at that whirlwind pattern. The traces of energy are terrifyingly deep."

"I couldn't replicate the forms, and the internal energy is even more beyond me..."

The rising talents following Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin.

There were about a dozen. Besides those in monk's robes and Taoist garments, many men and women wore silk, likely young clan members from Sichuan's martial families.

Some halted abruptly upon seeing Jeong Yeon-shin, while others couldn't tear their eyes from the martial art traces left by the Nine Sects seniors, or glared at the bloodied Five Heroes of Passion Protection.

Among them, a pair — a man and a woman — quickly composed themselves and took their places behind Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin.

A young Taoist and a young Buddhist nun. Both emanated formless energy waves, as if they had fully absorbed the spiritual essence of their sects.

"Fourth Uncle, now that we've caught them, we should return quickly. These men... aren't they mercenaries bought by the Thirteen Evil Sects? They might be carrying incense of submission."

The young Taoist spoke with sharp, focused eyes.

He addressed Taoist Master Qing Yin, one of the Five Great Sword Masters of Qingcheng Sect, as "Fourth Uncle." The young man must be a renowned rising talent of Sichuan's martial world in his own right.

The young Buddhist nun beside him nodded deeply, enough to cast a shadow beneath her high nose bridge.

"Such behavior isn't uncommon. There have been cases where they harm Desolate Fortress martial artists or righteous sect disciples and then flee into mountains with few people..."

Divine Nun Zi Wei, with the young nun standing behind her, cut in.

"Ten Perfections Sect, Golden Wing Sect, Pure Demon Alliance, Tyrant Sword Sect, and Yu Ling must all be involved. Which side do you think these men belong to?"

"I heard a new alliance leader has claimed the throne of the Pure Demon Alliance while Invincible was away. It would make sense for them to target masters from Emei and Qingcheng as bait. They need to prove themselves."

The young Buddhist nun, having given a lengthy answer, added a couple more thoughts.

"If the two of you are the targets, the Pure Demon Alliance Leader himself might come. Didn't he lose the Scorpion Sword Demon and Pure Demon Divine Hand to the Radiant Demon Wing Leader?"

Even as this grave discussion continued, the gazes of Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin never strayed from Jeong Yeon-shin.

They wore the look of people guarding against a formidable unknown.

Not all Nine Sects masters readily accepted Desolate Fortress. This was because many secular sects existed within their sphere.

Since numerous martial sects persecuted by the government traced their main lineage to the Nine Sects, Desolate Fortress and the Nine Sects sometimes clashed even when pursuing the same goals.

Jeong Yeon-shin had heard such stories from his maternal uncle, Ma Jin, when he succeeded to the position of Radiant Demon Wing Leader.

— You ask why I clashed with the Plum Blossom Sword Masters? Some of those causing sword trouble in civilian homes while claiming to be heroes were affiliated with Mount Hua.

When I tried to arrest them for questioning, the Sword Absolute appeared and spouted nonsense. Said they would interrogate their own people.

That was the reason the Radiant Demon Wing and Mount Hua's Plum Blossom Sword Masters had clashed before Jeong Yeon-shin entered Desolate Fortress.

Mount Hua was righteous yet true to its nature as a martial sect, while his maternal uncle, true to his alias of Harbinger of Death, never extended kind consideration to martial artists.

'Would they believe me if I revealed my identity?'

Jeong Yeon-shin considered.

Desolate Fortress's Radiant Demon Wing Leader was a pivotal figure in the martial world.

Anyone at the level of the Nine Sects' leadership would inevitably be aware of his distinguishing characteristics.

This would be especially true for Lightning Genius, the youngest Black-rank, known in Sichuan as the Flower Shadow King.

"Why do you hesitate to speak? The 'Desolate' character is written on your shoulder."

"If the characters are golden, aren't you a direct disciple of the Desolate Fortress Lord? Among the squad leaders under the Divine Sword Group, only Radiant Demon Wing Leader Lightning Genius bears that distinction, and I heard he's a boy of about seventeen..."

Taoist Master Qing Yin and his disciple-nephew spoke.

The martial artists at the highest echelons of this world were well-informed indeed. Extracting anything from them would not be easy.

"Surely he's not posing as the Flower Shadow King...?"

The young Buddhist nun's words never reached Jeong Yeon-shin's ears.

He was well-versed in etiquette and principles. Having received a Buddhist palm-press and a martial salute, he felt a return greeting should come first. He slowly raised both hands.

Rustle.

Jeong Yeon-shin was forming a respectful martial salute — the kind he would offer any monk or Taoist — when he hesitated.

It was difficult to accept those before him as a monk and a Taoist. The conduct that Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin had displayed earlier kept flickering through his mind.

They had clearly done the right thing, but their manner had been ugly. One might say they lacked dignity.

"I am the Radiant Demon Wing Leader."

He stopped midway through raising his hands and addressed them informally.
